methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $3.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: xAI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $250B

private.name: Crusoe Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B+

private.name: SandboxAQ
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $5.6B

private.name: WEKA

private.name: Modal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $1.1B

data.narrative:
Ayar Labs, an AI & Software company at Series E, is valued at $3.75B post-money following $500M in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises five same-sector, same-stage private companies (xAI, Crusoe Energy, SandboxAQ, WEKA, Modal) plus one public comparable (Palantir). The private peer set ranges from $1.1B to $250B in valuation.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the typical Series E-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.4x applied to the $3.8B last post-money, yielding $5.2B. Peer-Median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage private comparables produces a median valuation of $5.6B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $250B. The wide peer range reflects heterogeneity within frontier AI infrastructure, with xAI as a substantial outlier.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $4.5B (low) to $6.4B (high), with a median of $5.4B. This implies modest upside from the current $3.75B valuation. Key uncertainties include the applicability of the xAI comp given its materially higher stage and capital access, and whether Stage-Progression's 1.4x multiplier accurately reflects market conditions for Series E AI companies. The narrow peer count (n=5) and wide dispersion also limit conviction in the median estimate.
